title: quercus robur var. fastigiata
intro: Quercus robur var. fastigiata, also known as the fastigiate English oak, is a cultivar of the pedunculate oak tree, characterized by its narrow, upright growth habit and slender branches. The leaves of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur var. fastigiata have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
